title: Schoenus globulifer
taxonomy: Schoenus globulifer is classified within the family Cyperaceae, which includes sedges and rushes. This plant is a member of the genus Schoenus, a group of sedges that are often found in wetland environments. The family Cyperaceae is part of the order Poales, a large group of flowering plants that includes grasses and other monocots. The order Poales is within the class Liliopsida, which contains a diverse range of flowering plants. Schoenus globulifer's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other monocots and its distinctive sedge-like characteristics.

intro: Schoenus globulifer, also known as the globular sedge, is a type of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects.
